title: Just curious: has anyone done a build performance comparison between podman and docker?
body:
Just curious: has anyone done a build performance comparison between podman and docker?
I didn't find any relevant information

<!--

BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

comments.body: Hi @usernameisnull , Could please elaborate more, did you mean `podman build` vs `docker build` benchmarking ?

> Hi @usernameisnull , Could please elaborate more, did you mean `podman build` vs `docker build` benchmarking ?

yes, like all in root mode.
I know there are many influencing factors, we can make a comparison under the best configuration of the two, or under the general configuration
about ~15% slower than docker, I got it from #13226
I think benchmarking needs to happen on different parameters like `rootfull vs rootless` , `native overlay vs fuse-overlay` and maybe documented in the repo itself.
@usernameisnull Just curious are you hitting any bottlenecks while performing builds ?

> 



> @usernameisnull Just curious are you hitting any bottlenecks while performing builds ?

No, It's just that we are doing technical selection, one of the thing is the build speed

> I think benchmarking needs to happen on different parameters like `rootfull vs rootless` , `native overlay vs fuse-overlay` and maybe documented in the repo itself.

I know there are many influencing factors, we can make a comparison under the best configuration of the two, or under the general configuration

The comparison should be docker versus rootful buildah
Rootless Buildah is better for security but potentially requires fuse-overlay which will slow it down on older kernels.

(Not to mention slirp4netns versus native kernel networking - it your build needs to download anything of significant size, root will have a significant advantage)

I have an open card to do a performance blog on Podman vs Docker, I should try and get that done next week...
In rootless mode, you could use --network=host during builds, which would eliminate that issue.

Make sure to also compare against `docker buildx` just as you want to compare with `buildah` directly ?

Using `docker build` and `podman build` is good for compatibility but not always for performance...

> (Not to mention slirp4netns versus native kernel networking - it your build needs to download anything of significant size, root will have a significant advantage)
> 
> I have an open card to do a performance blog on Podman vs Docker, I should try and get that done next week...

very much looking forward to
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@usernameisnull @mheon @afbjorklund What should we do with this one?
Keep it open until my blog comes out. Probably next week?
+1 looking forward to it @mheon ! Please post the link
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@mheon I am guessing your blog came out, so closing.

@mheon Is this the blog then? https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/speeding-container-buildah
I couldn't find any other.
I am searching, and I can't actually *find* the blog published anywhere. I wrote it and submitted it, but it looks like it was never published? I'll just throw it up on the podman.io blog site.
